meta.certificationName: Microsoft Certified: Fabric Data Engineer Associate (DP-700)
meta.title: MS-FDE-1: Ingestion, Lakehouse, Real-Time Analytics and Governance

questions.stem: Which Fabric storage layer underpins lakehouses, warehouses, and other Fabric data experiences?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because OneLake is the unifying storage layer across Fabric experiences. **B** is incorrect because Data Activator is an event-driven action service. **C** is incorrect because Power Query M is a transformation language. **D** is incorrect because deployment pipelines manage releases. [Microsoft Fabric OneLake](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-overview)

questions.stem: Which Fabric item is designed to orchestrate Copy Data, Notebook, and Stored Procedure activities?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because data pipelines orchestrate multi-step data workflows. **B** is incorrect because semantic models serve analytics. **C** is incorrect because dashboards visualize results. **D** is incorrect because Capacity Metrics monitors usage. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: Which Fabric feature is best for low-code transformations using Power Query M?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Dataflows Gen2 are the low-code Power Query transformation surface. **B** is incorrect because Eventhouse is for real-time/KQL workloads. **C** is incorrect because warehouse views are SQL objects. **D** is incorrect because Git integration manages source control. [Dataflows Gen2 in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/dataflows-gen2-overview)

questions.stem: A team needs to perform large-scale custom transformations on Delta data with PySpark. Which Fabric capability fits best?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Spark notebooks are the main Fabric surface for scalable code-based transformation with PySpark. **B** is incorrect because deployment pipelines and labels do not execute transformations. **C** is incorrect because viewer is a permission role. [Use notebooks in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/how-to-use-notebook)

questions.stem: When choosing between a Lakehouse and a Warehouse in Fabric, which statement is most accurate?

options.text: Lakehouses are Spark-first over Delta files, while Warehouses are SQL-centric analytical stores
Lakehouses cannot expose a SQL endpoint

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Lakehouses emphasize Spark and Delta while Warehouses emphasize SQL warehousing. **B** is incorrect because Warehouses do use SQL. **C** is incorrect because Lakehouses do expose a SQL endpoint. **D** is incorrect because notebooks are not restricted to Warehouses. [What is a lakehouse in Microsoft Fabric?](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview)

questions.stem: What does the Fabric Capacity Metrics app primarily help a data engineer monitor?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because the metrics app helps track workload consumption and throttling. **B** is incorrect because it is not limited to syntax, endorsements, or membership. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/enterprise/metrics-app)

questions.stem: Which Delta maintenance actions are commonly used in Fabric Lakehouses to improve file layout and clean up old files?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because `OPTIMIZE` improves file layout and `VACUUM` removes obsolete files. **B** is incorrect because the other commands serve different purposes. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [What is a lakehouse in Microsoft Fabric?](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview)

questions.stem: A Fabric pipeline must ingest data incrementally from a source system. Which pattern is most appropriate?

options.text: Track watermarks or change data and load only new or changed records
Reload the entire source every minute forever

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because incremental patterns rely on watermarks or CDC markers to avoid full reloads. **B** is incorrect because constant full reloads are wasteful. **C** is incorrect because monitoring does not implement incrementality. **D** is incorrect because endorsement labels are unrelated. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: What is the Fabric Lakehouse SQL analytics endpoint used for?

options.text: Read-only SQL queries over lakehouse tables

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because the SQL analytics endpoint provides read-only SQL access to lakehouse data. **B** is incorrect because it is not the streaming ingestion service or admin/publishing surface. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [SQL analytics endpoint for lakehouses](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-sql-analytics-endpoint)

questions.stem: What is the main value of right-sizing Fabric capacities?

options.text: Balancing performance and cost based on actual workload demand
Removing the need for Delta optimization

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because capacity sizing is a core cost/performance tradeoff in Fabric. **B** is incorrect because it does not replace security, optimization, or orchestration decisions. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/enterprise/metrics-app)

questions.stem: A Fabric warehouse is usually preferred over a lakehouse when the primary consumer needs which experience?

options.text: A SQL-centric warehousing experience with T-SQL DDL/DML

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because warehouses are optimized for SQL-centric warehousing. **B** is incorrect because lakehouses suit Spark notebooks. **C** is incorrect because Power Query and KQL are different experiences. **D** is incorrect because not applicable. [Data Warehouse in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-warehouse/data-warehousing)

questions.stem: Which Fabric real-time item ingests and routes streaming events from sources such as Event Hubs or Kafka?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Eventstream is the Fabric item for capturing and routing real-time events. **B** is incorrect because Dataflows are batch/Power Query transformations. **C** is incorrect because deployment pipelines and semantic models do not ingest event streams. [Eventstream in Real-Time Intelligence](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/real-time-intelligence/event-streams/overview)

questions.stem: Which query language is primarily used with Fabric Eventhouse data?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Eventhouse is designed around KQL for querying event and log-style data. **B** is incorrect because DAX and M serve different layers. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [Kusto Query Language overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-explorer/kusto/query/)

questions.stem: What is the main role of an Eventhouse in Fabric?

options.text: Store and query high-frequency real-time data

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Eventhouse stores and serves real-time analytical data with KQL. **B** is incorrect because it is not a workbook, repo, or refresh-only artifact. [Eventhouse overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/real-time-intelligence/eventhouse)

questions.stem: Why would a data engineer configure pipeline alerts or notifications?

options.text: To detect failures or SLA risks quickly
To improve OneLake storage capacity automatically

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because alerts improve operational awareness and incident response. **B** is incorrect because they do not resize storage, replace quality checks, or create shortcuts. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: A team wants to reference raw files already stored in ADLS Gen2 without copying them into a new Fabric location. Which feature is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because OneLake shortcuts let Fabric reference supported external storage without copying data. **B** is incorrect because the other choices do not virtualize external files. [OneLake shortcuts](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-shortcuts)

questions.stem: What is a key advantage of connecting a Fabric workspace to GitHub or Azure DevOps?

options.text: It supports version control and collaboration for Fabric items
It increases Spark executor memory automatically

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Git integration adds version control and collaborative change management. **B** is incorrect because it does not directly resize compute or replace permissions/deployment features. [Git integration in Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/cicd/git-integration/intro-to-git-integration)

questions.stem: Which workspace role is most appropriate for a user who must create and edit content but does not need full workspace administration?

options.text: Contributor or Member, depending the exact governance needs

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Contributor/Member roles allow content work without full workspace administration. **B** is incorrect because Viewer is too limited and tenant admin is far broader. [Roles in workspaces in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/fundamentals/roles-workspaces)

questions.stem: What is the main value of Microsoft Purview integration with Fabric?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Purview complements Fabric with governance, catalog, and lineage features. **B** is incorrect because it does not replace storage or execute code. [Microsoft Purview and Fabric integration](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/governance/microsoft-purview-fabric)

questions.stem: Why might a data engineer use medallion design in Fabric?

options.text: To separate raw, refined, and curated data responsibilities across layers
To eliminate the need for security
To force all queries through KQL
To prevent SQL access to data

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because medallion architecture separates concerns across bronze/silver/gold style layers. **B** is incorrect because it does not replace security or force a query language. [What is a lakehouse in Microsoft Fabric?](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview)

questions.stem: A source system occasionally sends late updates. Which incremental design handles this more safely than using only a monotonically increasing ID?

options.text: Use a watermark or change-tracking column that captures updates as well as inserts

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because change-tracking or watermarks that reflect updates capture late changes more reliably than insert-only IDs. **B** is incorrect because ignoring updates risks missing data. **C** is incorrect because dashboards and endorsements are unrelated. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: What is the main benefit of collecting Delta statistics and maintaining table layout?

options.text: Improved query planning and data skipping opportunities

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because healthy table statistics and file layout help the engine prune and plan efficiently. **B** is incorrect because they do not generate RLS, eliminate cost, or replace BI models. [What is a lakehouse in Microsoft Fabric?](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview)

questions.stem: Which KQL operator family is commonly used for filtering and aggregating event data?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because `where` filters and `summarize` aggregates in KQL. **B** is incorrect because the other choices are not the core KQL analysis operators here. [Kusto Query Language overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-explorer/kusto/query/)

questions.stem: What is the purpose of Activator in Fabric real-time solutions?

options.text: Trigger actions or alerts from event conditions

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Activator enables event-driven actions and alerts based on streaming conditions. **B** is incorrect because it does not transform data, store Delta tables, or replace KQL. [Eventstream in Real-Time Intelligence](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/real-time-intelligence/event-streams/overview)

questions.stem: A workspace contains both sensitive engineering notebooks and broadly shared BI reports. Which approach best enforces least privilege?

options.text: Use appropriate workspace/item permissions and share only the required items
Store everything in a single public app

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because least privilege comes from careful role assignment and item-level sharing choices. **B** is incorrect because broad admin access and public exposure violate the goal. **C** is incorrect because lineage is not the issue. [Roles in workspaces in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/fundamentals/roles-workspaces)

questions.stem: Which Fabric feature records past pipeline executions for troubleshooting?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because run history provides operational visibility for troubleshooting pipeline outcomes. **B** is incorrect because the other options are unrelated to orchestration history. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: Why might a Fabric engineer choose a notebook over Dataflows Gen2 for a transformation step?

options.text: The transformation requires custom large-scale Spark logic
Notebooks are the only feature that can be scheduled
Notebooks are required for all SQL

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because notebooks are better for custom scalable code-based processing. **B** is incorrect because scheduling is not exclusive to notebooks. **C** is incorrect because Dataflows can read data. **D** is incorrect because notebooks are not required for all SQL. [Use notebooks in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/how-to-use-notebook)

questions.stem: Which statement about lakehouse tables in Fabric is accurate?

options.text: They are based on Delta table concepts and can be discovered by the lakehouse automatically
They cannot be queried with SQL

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Fabric lakehouse tables are Delta-based and automatically surfaced in the lakehouse experience. **B** is incorrect because they can be queried with SQL and are not dashboard/Eventhouse artifacts. [What is a lakehouse in Microsoft Fabric?](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/lakehouse-overview)

questions.stem: A notebook job is slow. Which Fabric surface is most useful for investigating stages, DAGs, and executor behavior?

options.text: Spark monitoring details for the notebook run

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Spark monitoring surfaces execution details such as stages and executor behavior. **B** is incorrect because the other choices do not diagnose Spark runtime performance. [Use notebooks in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/how-to-use-notebook)

questions.stem: What is the main effect of sensitivity labels in Fabric?

options.text: They help classify and govern content based on data sensitivity

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because sensitivity labels support governance and classification. **B** is incorrect because they do not create checkpoints or replace access controls. [Microsoft Purview and Fabric integration](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/governance/microsoft-purview-fabric)

questions.stem: Why would a team use retry policies in Fabric pipelines?

options.text: To handle transient source or network failures automatically

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because retry policies increase resilience for transient failures. **B** is incorrect because they are not substitutes for validation, DAX tuning, or workspace creation. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: Which Fabric item is best suited to low-latency dashboards directly over real-time event data?

options.text: A Real-Time Dashboard backed by Eventhouse

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Real-Time Dashboards are designed for low-latency visualization over event data. **B** is incorrect because the other choices are not visualization surfaces for streaming analytics. [Eventhouse overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/real-time-intelligence/eventhouse)

questions.stem: A team wants business analysts to join curated warehouse tables and lakehouse tables in a single analytical query path. Which Fabric capability makes this type of mixed analytical design possible?

options.text: Fabric's shared platform with warehouse/lakehouse interoperability

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Fabric supports interoperable analytics across its storage experiences, enabling lakehouse and warehouse scenarios in one platform. **B** is incorrect because the other options do not provide this analytical capability. [Data Warehouse in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-warehouse/data-warehousing)

questions.stem: Which role is most appropriate for a user who only needs to view workspace content?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Viewer is the read-oriented role for consuming content without authoring or administration. **B** is incorrect because the others provide broader permissions. [Roles in workspaces in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/fundamentals/roles-workspaces)

questions.stem: Which transformation surface is based on Power Query rather than Spark?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Dataflows Gen2 are Power Query based. **B** is incorrect because notebooks are Spark-based for this context. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [Dataflows Gen2 in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/dataflows-gen2-overview)

questions.stem: What is the most likely consequence of ignoring repeated capacity throttling warnings?

options.text: Performance degradation and delayed workloads may continue

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because persistent throttling can impact workload responsiveness and SLAs. **B** is incorrect because it does not directly disable security, delete data, or erase history. [Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/enterprise/metrics-app)

questions.stem: Which TWO Fabric capabilities are most directly involved in a real-time ingestion and action pipeline? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because Eventstream captures/routes the data and Activator responds to event conditions with actions or alerts. **C** is incorrect because deployment pipelines manage content promotion rather than stream ingestion/actions. **D** is incorrect because calculation groups are semantic-model metadata. [Eventstream in Real-Time Intelligence](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/real-time-intelligence/event-streams/overview)

questions.stem: Which TWO features best support repeatable batch ingestion and transformation in Fabric? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because data pipelines orchestrate the workflow and notebooks handle scalable custom transformations. **C** is incorrect because endorsement labels communicate trust rather than execute ETL. **D** is incorrect because RLS is a data-access control, not a batch-engineering feature. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: Which TWO governance features most directly improve discoverability and compliance in Fabric? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because Purview lineage/catalog and sensitivity labels strengthen governance and compliance practices. **C** is incorrect because disabling Git does not improve governance by itself. **D** is incorrect because removing roles would weaken access control. [Microsoft Purview and Fabric integration](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/governance/microsoft-purview-fabric)

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about OneLake shortcuts are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: They can point to supported external storage such as ADLS Gen2
They replace all source-system permissions automatically
They are only for Eventhouse data

questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because shortcuts avoid unnecessary copying and can reference supported external storage locations. **C** is incorrect because source permissions and secure access still matter. **D** is incorrect because shortcuts are not limited to Eventhouse scenarios. [OneLake shortcuts](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-shortcuts)

questions.stem: Which TWO KQL operators are foundational for shaping event data during analysis? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because `extend` adds calculated columns and `project` selects/reshapes columns in KQL workflows. **C** is incorrect because `grant` is an access-control statement, not the core data-shaping operator here. **D** is incorrect because `vacuum` is unrelated to KQL. [Kusto Query Language overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-explorer/kusto/query/)

questions.stem: Which TWO actions are most likely to improve cost and performance hygiene for Fabric engineering workloads? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because capacity sizing plus ongoing monitoring are practical optimization foundations. **C** is incorrect because lineage is a governance feature and disabling it does not improve hygiene. **D** is incorrect because avoiding maintenance generally hurts performance and data hygiene. [Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/enterprise/metrics-app)

questions.stem: Which TWO access controls are most important when a user should edit a specific item but not administer the full workspace? (Select TWO)

options.text: Making the user a tenant admin

questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because granular permissions combined with least-privilege roles let users work on the needed item without broad administrative access. **C** is incorrect because tenant admin is far too broad. **D** is incorrect because public publishing bypasses least-privilege principles. [Roles in workspaces in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/fundamentals/roles-workspaces)

questions.stem: Which TWO techniques most directly support robust incremental ingestion in Fabric? (Select TWO)

options.text: Reload the full source each minute
Rely on endorsement labels to mark processed rows

questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because watermarks/CDC handle which data to ingest and monitoring helps operate the flow reliably. **C** is incorrect because constant full reloads are inefficient and unnecessary for incremental design. **D** is incorrect because endorsement labels are not ingestion state markers. [Data pipelines in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-factory/pipeline-overview)

questions.stem: Why are OneLake shortcuts useful in medallion-style Fabric designs?

options.text: They allow teams to reference existing raw data locations without duplicating files
They turn all tables into Eventhouse tables

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because shortcuts reduce duplication while preserving access to existing data locations. **B** is incorrect because they do not replace security, convert platforms, or block SQL access. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [OneLake shortcuts](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-shortcuts)

questions.stem: Which Fabric capability is best when an engineer needs to inspect and debug PySpark code interactively during development?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because notebook sessions are the interactive environment for authoring and debugging PySpark. **B** is incorrect because the other choices are governance or release features. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [Use notebooks in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/data-engineering/how-to-use-notebook)

questions.stem: What is the main reason to keep item-level sharing distinct from workspace-wide roles in Fabric?

options.text: It supports more granular least-privilege access
It removes the need for RLS

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because item-level sharing helps grant only the access that is needed. **B** is incorrect because it does not affect retention, lineage availability, or eliminate row-level security. [Roles in workspaces in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/fundamentals/roles-workspaces)

questions.stem: Which real-time Fabric artifact is primarily a queryable store rather than an ingestion router?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Eventhouse stores and serves real-time data for KQL analysis, whereas Eventstream routes incoming events. **B** is incorrect because deployment pipelines and Dataflows are different surfaces. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [Eventhouse overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/real-time-intelligence/eventhouse)

questions.stem: Why should Fabric engineers review both capacity metrics and Spark/pipeline run details together?

options.text: They provide complementary views of platform pressure and workload-level behavior
Either one alone always explains every issue
They are required to create shortcuts

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because capacity views show shared resource pressure while run details show workload-specific symptoms. **B** is incorrect because a single view rarely explains every issue. **C** is incorrect because they do not replace governance or enable shortcuts. [Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/enterprise/metrics-app)

questions.stem: Which KQL operator is used to derive a calculated column during query execution?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because `extend` adds calculated columns in KQL queries. **B** is incorrect because the other commands are not used for this query-shaping purpose. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [Kusto Query Language overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/data-explorer/kusto/query/)
